climate.description: Mild late-summer conditions ideal for ultra, starting cool/green in Cantabria, warming to dry Mediterranean south.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 73. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 70.

course_description.character: A grand traverse of Spain from the wild Cantabrian north to Valencia's Mediterranean warmth, blending relentless climbs, picturesque villages, and diverse terrains through mountains, plateaus, and coasts.
course_description.signature_challenge: Overcoming the cumulative 35,000ft elevation across 621 miles, punctuated by Spain's most scenic passes and cultural gems.
suffering_zones: Portillo de la Lunada: Iconic 30km Cantabrian pass with steep gradients and exposed high-altitude scenery, a Vuelta a España regular.
Iberian Plateau: Arid, rolling expanses demanding sustained power and heat management midway.
Puerto de Almedijar & Oronet: Final Valencian Sierra climbs testing fatigued legs with technical descents to the coast.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Sleeping areas at living bases; Santander offers hotels near start/finish venue.
logistics.transport: Semi-autonomous ultra with dedicated support: PGO tracking, living bases for rest/refuel, bib collection, briefings, and awards at Palacio de los Deportes in Santander.

history.origin_story: New flagship event in Race Across Series, launching Spain's ultra-cycling scene with inaugural 2025/2026 editions drawing international talent.

final_verdict.one_liner: Spain's ultimate road ultra odyssey: 621 miles of mountains, culture, and unbreakable spirit.
